meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Master a high-velocity client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your MVP faster and start generating revenue today.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of rigorous scope management. By implementing a standardized client workflow, you decouple feature creep from development. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated iteration, ensuring your MVP remains focused, functional, and ready for market launch within days.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost finished' projects—brilliant ideas strangled by feature creep and the paralyzing fog of infinite iteration. You didn't become a founder to spend six months in local development; you became one to solve problems and capture value. The difference between a hobbyist and a founder is the ability to ship.

To break this cycle, you must treat your development process like a production line. By integrating a disciplined client workflow with AI-assisted engineering, you force yourself to prioritize the 'must-haves' that generate revenue. It is time to stop tinkering and start building a product that actually hits the market.

steps.title: Define the Revenue-Generating Core
steps.description: Strip your project down to the one feature that solves a burning problem. If a feature doesn't directly lead to a user paying, it gets cut.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this SaaS idea and generate a minimal viable schema that focuses strictly on the core transaction loop. Exclude all non-essential administrative features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate core database schema for [Project Name] focusing only on user auth and core transaction'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use automated tooling to build the skeleton of your application so you aren't staring at a blank IDE.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust boilerplate for a SaaS MVP using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include authentication and a basic dashboard shell.

steps.title: Implement the 'Gatekeeper' Workflow
steps.description: Establish a rule where no code is written without a pre-defined user story and acceptance criteria.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft an acceptance criteria template for my next 3 features. Keep them focused on user conversion and speed to market.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Add feature: [Feature Name] with verified acceptance criteria'

steps.title: Automate Iteration with Claude Code
steps.description: Stop debugging manually. Let AI handle the boilerplate and repetitive logic while you review the architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current codebase to improve modularity and prepare for API integration. Ensure error handling is production-ready.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement API integration for Stripe checkout with robust error handling'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Path
steps.description: Automate the path to production so your code is always in a deployable state, preventing the 'big bang' release failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline script that runs tests and deploys to Vercel upon every successful merge to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup github actions for automated deployment to production'

pro_tips: Always build for the 'happy path' first; edge cases are revenue killers in the MVP stage.
Use Claude Code to write tests before features to force yourself to define success criteria upfront.
Implement a 'Feature Kill Switch' in your dashboard to disable incomplete modules without rolling back code.
Never let a coding session exceed 90 minutes without a git commit; momentum is your most valuable asset.
Treat your database schema as the source of truth for your business logic to avoid spaghetti code.
content.next_steps: Once your core transaction loop is functional, push to production immediately. Do not wait for design perfection—get real user feedback on your MVP today.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the strategy to stop stalling and start scaling. Stop building for a future that doesn't exist and start shipping for the customers who need your solution right now. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
